expected_properties: When a and b are both set to the maximum N‑bit value, c wraps around to (2^N‑2) modulo 2^N, demonstrating correct overflow handling.
additional_cases.goal: Confirm that the adder truncates overflow bits and produces the expected modular result.
notes: Uses the same combinational observation model as the contract.
preconditions: Inputs a and b are driven with the maximum representable value (all bits = 1).
stimulus_intent: Drive both a and b with the maximum N‑bit value to provoke overflow.
timing_assumptions: Check c after inputs settle; no fixed latency is assumed.

expected_properties: Successive independent input vectors produce corresponding independent output values without residual effects.
additional_cases.goal: Verify that rapid successive changes on a and b produce correct, independent outputs each time.
notes: Even though the design is combinational, this case checks for any unintended latch or state retention.
preconditions: A sequence of distinct input pairs is applied with sufficient settling time between them.
stimulus_intent: Apply a series of varied (a,b) pairs back‑to‑back and monitor c after each change.
timing_assumptions: Each output is observed after the corresponding inputs have stabilized.

expected_properties: Output c equals (a + b) modulo 2^N for all legal input combinations.
baseline_case_enrichments.goal: Validate functional correctness of the adder across the full input space without assuming timing.
notes: Assumes purely combinational behavior as indicated by the contract.
No internal state or latency is inferred.
stimulus_intent: Drive a wide variety of random legal values on inputs a and b, covering both typical and extreme patterns.
timing_assumptions: Observe c after inputs have settled; no fixed-cycle latency is assumed.

expected_properties: Output c correctly reflects overflow behavior (wrap‑around) when adding maximum operand values.
baseline_case_enrichments.goal: Exercise boundary conditions that may trigger overflow or sign‑bit changes.
notes: Focuses on min/max patterns and zero/one patterns for inputs a and b.
stimulus_intent: Apply zero, one, maximum, and just‑below‑maximum values to a and b.
timing_assumptions: Observe c after inputs have stabilized; no latency assumptions are made.
